reasoning_messages.reasoning:
We need to propose plan: We are on Rocky Linux 10. Need to set ocredit = -1 in pwquality.conf. Need to avoid deletion of commented lines. Better to comment out existing uncommented lines and append new line at end. Ensure backup.

We must use read_file before modifying. Use sed -i to modify options in place. We have multiple conf file locations: /etc/security/pwquality.conf and possibly .conf in .d.

We should target /etc/security/pwquality.conf and any included .d/*.conf. For each file, read content, backup, use sed to replace lines where ^\s*ocredit\s*=?(.*) with ocredit = -1, ensure uncommented. If commented, leave them.

Also can just add a new file in .d to set. But simpler: modify main config.

Implement plan: backup config to pwquality.conf.bak if not already. Then read file. Use sed -i to replace line.

But the review: we shouldn't delete commented lines. So plan: uncomment any commented ocredit lines: replace '#\s*ocredit' with 'ocredit='. Then set to -1. Or if no line, append.

Let's craft plan: We will create a backup of /etc/security/pwquality.conf (if not exists). Then using sed, uncomment any '#\s*ocredit' lines and set to -1 by replacing entire line. If still no line after that, append at EOF 'ocredit = -1'.

We need read_file before modifying to confirm.

Also ensure we don't add duplicate lines: if line exists, modify only one.

We'll adopt approach: Use sed command in two passes: first, replace '^\s*#?\s*ocredit\s*=\s*.*' with 'ocredit = -1' (uncomment and set). That ensures any existing line (commented or not) replaced. But this would modify each occurrence. We need ensure only first? Could do 's/^\s*#?\s*ocredit\s*=\s*.*$/ocredit = -1/' which will modify first? Actually sed default processes all; we could limit with '0,/ocredit/' but easier: just modifications. If we accidentally modify multiple, that's okay? Might create multiple lines; better to remove duplicates.

Better: delete all ocredit lines, then append single line. That would keep commented lines? They might be comments. But requirement: keep commented lines for context. We can comment out previous line by prefixing '# ' and append new line.

Thus approach: For each file, read content. Then use sed to comment out lines matching ^\s*ocredit\s*=\s*.*. Replace with '# \0'. Then append 'ocredit = -1' at end.

This preserves commented lines but showcases new.

Better: use sed -i '/^\s*ocredit\s*=\s*.*$/ s/^/# /' file. Then echo to file.

Implementation: We'll get file path, backup.

In commands: read_file, run_cmd for sed, run_cmd for echo >> file.

Implement for main config and any .conf in .d.

We must consider commands individually, one at a time. Might have several commands.

The plan: 1) backup main config. 2) comment out existing lines via sed. 3) append new line. 4) repeat for each .d file: backup, comment out lines, append.

We also need to pay attention to systemctl? Not needed.

Ok provide plan description. Then review_plan. Let's do.
